name: Roast Loin of Pork with Southwestern Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds pork loin, boned and tied
1 large garlic clove, peeled and halved
1 medium red bell pepper, finely diced
1 medium green bell pepper, finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the pork with the cut garlic and discard the garlic. Place the pork on a rack in a roasting pan.
2. Remove the stems from two ancho chilies and place them in a blender. Chop and grind to a fairly fine powder. Transfer this to a small mixing bowl.
3. Stir in the chili powder, cayenne, oregano, thyme, cumin, garlic powder, coriander, and celery salt. Mix until completely combined. Remove a half cup of the mixture for sauce and rub the remainder all over the roast.
4. Set the roast in the oven at 475F and cook for 30 minutes. Transfer the meat to a wire rack to cool for 30 minutes. It will still be raw in the center, but cooking is finished later; this cooling period ensures even cooking and tender meat. Reduce the oven temperature to 375F.
5. While the roast cooks, prepare the sauce. Render fat from bacon in a large skillet over low heat. Remove the bacon and reserve it. Drain all but two tablespoons of the fat. Add minced garlic and sauté for one minute. Add red, green peppers, poblano, ancho chili, tomatoes, and onion. Sprinkle reserved spice mixture over vegetables and cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until soft, about 15 minutes. Pour in red wine and cook until reduced to a glaze. Pour in veal stock and continue cooking over medium heat until the liquid is reduced by half, about 15 minutes. Crumble reserved bacon into the sauce. Keep warm while finishing roast.
6. Return the pork roast to the oven at 375F and cook for 20-25 minutes until a meat thermometer inserted in the center reads 137 degrees. Remove from oven and transfer to a rack. Let cool slightly before slicing into medallions.
7. Serve roast on a pool of veggie sauce with corn pudding on side.
